note: ICP filter BUGFIX (atlas_icp_filter.py). Fixes a real defect: the prior version wrote lifecycle='icp_fail'/'icp_keep', which VIOLATES the live lifecycle CHECK constraint {imported,queued,enriched,ai_enriched,verified,rejected} (PG 18.4, confirmed seq-7-diag) and would abort the sweep. Patched to write a DEDICATED, unconstrained icp_status column (added additively, no change to existing columns). ALSO removes the old hard-DELETE prune path entirely -- removal is now delegated to atlas_cold_archive.py (reversible). First apply = fetch+sha-verify + --selftest + DRY-RUN sweep (reports keep/fail, writes nothing). To enforce tagging: re-run with ATLAS_ICP_DRYRUN=0. [fix: removed env-var prefix on the sweep step so it passes the run_allowlisted allowlist; dry-run is the script default]

steps.note: ICP filter fix: icp_status column (not CHECK-constrained lifecycle); no hard delete. Supersedes the earlier ICP manifest's lifecycle writes + prune. Safe additive schema; tag-only.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/importers/atlas_icp_filter.py':'83468be70ef780a881e4eba4952bcd1e843d93e0cee14c5e5e72f8488f37cd65'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
